agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. Subject to healthcare compliance requirements around data access, record integrity, and provider credentialing. The domain encodes scheduling, credentialing, access control, and record immutability rules.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled consultation between exactly one patient and one licensed physician, defined by a start time and end time."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A medical provider whose professional license number has been verified by the platform and who is therefore eligible to accept bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A state violation where two or more appointments with overlapping time windows are assigned to the same physician."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period ending 2 hours before an appointment's start time, during which the patient retains self-service cancellation rights."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform operator with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments outside the patient cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment; the only physician authorized to view that appointment's associated patient medical records."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. Subject to a time-based immutability lock."},{"term":"Note Lock","definition":"An irreversible transition of consultation notes to a read-only state, triggered automatically 48 hours after the appointment end time."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The credentialing process that confirms a physician's license number is valid. A prerequisite for the physician to appear as bookable on the platform."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient may only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unverified providers to treat patients is a compliance and patient-safety violation. The system is in a corrupt state if this occurs.","testable_assertion":"For every appointment in state BOOKED or CONFIRMED, the associated physician's license_verified flag must be true."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments with overlapping time windows.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking is a physical constraint — a physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. Violating this corrupts scheduling integrity.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two non-cancelled appointments may have time ranges [start, end) that overlap."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their own appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., to 1 hour or 4 hours) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request is rejected when now() > appointment.start_time - 2 hours."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A staff administrator may cancel any appointment at any time, including within the 2-hour window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a role-based policy granting override capability; the threshold and role scope are adjustable business decisions.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a user with role STAFF_ADMIN succeeds regardless of proximity to appointment start time."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before the physician can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Credentialing is a regulatory gate. An unverified physician existing in the bookable pool is a compliance violation and a data integrity issue.","testable_assertion":"A physician with license_verified = false must not appear in booking search results and must be rejected if referenced in a booking request."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records for a given appointment are visible only to the patient and the treating physician of that appointment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Medical record access control is a healthcare privacy mandate (analogous to HIPAA). Unauthorized access constitutes a data breach — the system is in a compromised state.","testable_assertion":"Any data access request for patient medical records must be denied unless the requester is the patient OR the physician assigned to the relevant appointment."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become permanently read-only 48 hours after the appointment end time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Clinical record immutability after a grace period is a compliance and audit integrity requirement. If notes can be modified after the lock window, the medical record is untrustworthy.","testable_assertion":"Any write or update request to consultation notes where now() > appointment.end_time + 48 hours must be rejected, regardless of the requester's role."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and an available time slot","when":"A patient submits a booking request for that physician and time slot","then":"The appointment is created successfully and the physician's schedule reflects the new booking"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The system rejects the booking with an error indicating the physician is not available for booking"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician with an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A second patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The system rejects the booking with a conflict error, preserving the original appointment"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A physician with an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"A second patient attempts to book the same physician for 10:30 to 11:00","then":"The system accepts the booking because the time ranges are adjacent, not overlapping"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with a booked appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation succeeds and the physician's time slot becomes available again"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A patient with a booked appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation and informs the patient to contact a staff administrator"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A booked appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"A staff administrator requests cancellation of that appointment","then":"The cancellation succeeds regardless of the cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A completed appointment with consultation notes, and the current time is 24 hours after appointment end","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully because the 48-hour lock window has not elapsed"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A completed appointment with consultation notes, and the current time is 49 hours after appointment end","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The system rejects the edit, indicating the notes are locked and read-only"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A patient with an appointment treated by Physician A","when":"Physician B (not the treating physician) requests access to that patient's medical records for that appointment","then":"The system denies access"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A patient with an appointment treated by Physician A","when":"The patient requests access to their own medical records","then":"Access is granted"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A patient with an appointment treated by Physician A","when":"Physician A requests access to the patient's medical records for that appointment","then":"Access is granted"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged for review or cancellation by a staff administrator. The physician must be immediately removed from the bookable pool. Past completed appointments remain valid historical records."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to book an appointment exactly at the 2-hour cancellation boundary","expected_behavior":"The boundary condition 'up to 2 hours before' must be enforced consistently. At exactly T-2h, the patient can still cancel. At T-1h59m59s, they cannot. The boundary should be clearly defined as >= 2 hours."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are being actively edited when the 48-hour lock triggers","expected_behavior":"The system should reject the save if the lock has engaged by the time the write is committed. The lock is based on wall-clock time, not edit-session start time."},{"scenario":"Staff administrator attempts to edit (not cancel) an appointment's consultation notes after the lock period","expected_behavior":"The note lock applies universally — even staff administrators cannot modify consultation notes after 48 hours. The lock is role-agnostic for writes."},{"scenario":"A physician has back-to-back appointments and one runs over its scheduled end time","expected_behavior":"The scheduling system enforces time-slot boundaries, not actual consultation duration. The overlap check is against booked slots, not real-time session activity."},{"scenario":"A patient is treated by multiple physicians across separate appointments","expected_behavior":"Each physician sees only the records associated with their own appointment(s) with that patient. Physician A cannot see records from Physician B's appointment, even for the same patient."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect double-booking wastes physician time and creates patient dissatisfaction leading to churn. Compliance violations (unauthorized record access, mutable locked notes) risk regulatory fines.","operational":"License verification gating ensures only credentialed providers operate on the platform, reducing legal exposure. The cancellation window policy balances patient flexibility with physician schedule stability.","user":"Patients gain confidence that their records are private and their physicians are verified. The 2-hour cancellation window gives reasonable flexibility while protecting physicians from last-minute no-shows.","risk":"Primary risks are HIPAA-equivalent privacy violations from improper record access controls, and audit trail corruption if note immutability is not enforced. Both carry regulatory and reputational risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Direct beneficiaries of privacy controls, license verification, and cancellation flexibility. Trust in the platform depends on correct enforcement of these rules."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Protected from double-booking and last-minute cancellations. Subject to credentialing gate. Responsible for completing notes within the 48-hour window."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Hold override authority for cancellations. Must be properly authenticated and audited given their elevated privileges."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Depend on record access controls and note immutability for regulatory audit readiness."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Bears liability for compliance failures. Scheduling integrity directly affects operational throughput and revenue."}]},"confidence":{"level":75,"reasoning":"The specification is clear and internally consistent. All rules are explicitly stated with no contradictions. Minor ambiguities exist around boundary conditions and edge cases (e.g., license revocation handling, exact overlap boundary semantics) but these are implementation-level clarifications rather than fundamental domain gaps.","high_confidence_areas":["Physician license verification as a booking prerequisite","No-overlap scheduling invariant","Patient vs. staff administrator cancellation authority split","Medical record access restricted to patient and treating physician","48-hour note lock rule"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'treating physician' grants access to all of a patient's records or only records from their specific appointment — assumed per-appointment scoping","Whether the 48-hour note lock can ever be overridden by any role (e.g., compliance audit correction) — assumed no override exists","What happens to existing bookings if a physician's license is revoked mid-schedule — not specified","Whether the 2-hour boundary is inclusive or exclusive (>= 2h or > 2h)"],"assumptions":["Medical record visibility is scoped per-appointment, not globally per patient-physician relationship","The 48-hour note lock is absolute and cannot be overridden by any role","Appointment time boundaries use half-open intervals [start, end) for overlap detection","The 2-hour cancellation window uses a >= comparison (exactly 2 hours still permits patient cancellation)"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. Subject to healthcare compliance requirements around data access, record integrity, and provider credentialing. The domain encodes scheduling, credentialing, access control, and record immutability rules.","bounded_context":"Appointment Scheduling & Clinical Records","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled consultation between exac

content.thinking:
Analyzing a telemedicine platform domain with clear rules from product and compliance teams.

Let me break down the rules:
1. Patient can only book with a licensed physician
2. No double-booking physicians (overlapping appointments)
3. Cancellation rules: patient can cancel up to 2h before; after that only staff admin
4. Physician license must be verified before accepting bookings
5. Medical records visible only to treating physician and patient
6. Consultation notes locked (read-only) 48h after appointment ends

These rules are fairly clear. No contradictions. Let me classify: no escalation needed, or minor ambiguities at most.


Now I'm thinking through some edge cases that could use clarification. If a patient sees multiple physicians, does each one see only their own records or the full history? What counts as overlap—can appointments be back-to-back or do they need gaps? Who's responsible for verifying licenses, and can admins unlock consultation notes after the 48-hour window? These are minor gaps in the spec, nothing that blocks implementation, so I'd estimate around 72-75% confidence overall.
